query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "dopd: dual on-policy distillation" computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on applying distillation to a specific downstream task (harmful meme detection) rather than addressing the core research question of extending the "Dual On-policy Distillation" (DOPD) algorithmic framework itself. While it uses the keyword "distillation," it does not measure the same mechanism, establish a baseline for DOPD, or provide the foundational methodology for the specific "Dual On-policy" approach the user is investigating, falling under the rejection rule for distinct
